test_refs: independent: validate-graph-schema.py on eval-log/dev-graph/live-trial-fixtures/r3-decompose graph + graph_node_id-drop negative control
IN1.observed: validate-graph-schema.py against the r3-decompose repo-root returned valid=true, violations=[], implementation_readiness=complete (0 missing required keys), exit 0. A negative control that removed graph_node_id from node[0] flipped the same script to valid=false with violations code=missing_id and code=schema_violation detail "$: 'graph_node_id' is a required property", proving the pre-send gate rejects any missing required key. Both pytest nodes passed (2 passed in ~1.6s).

test_refs: independent: r3 tmux live run transcript.jsonl (145 records, model claude-opus-4-6) + pane.txt + out/status.json
OUT1.observed: The r3 live run (Claude Code v2.1.62, Opus 4.6, bypassPermissions, ~9m self-driven, 0 nudges/0 gate responses) invoked dev-graph:run-dev-graph-decompose with --dry-run on the macro-positive scenario. The transcript shows the skill genuinely executed resolve-repo-context.py, computed the pre-existing graph_digest e49f6b1f... with node_count=1, ran GOAL-SEEK VERIFICATION: PASS, and emitted only draft preview with no local/Beads/GitHub/Projects write; the source graph digest stayed unchanged (no direct C02 add). out/status.json recorded {"status":"PASS","scenario":"decompose-macro-positive"} and the agent ended DONE: PASS. verdict.json overall=PASS, goal_verdict=PASS, tier=live, downgrade_reason=null, transcript_sha256 52fbefc2..., skill_dir_tree_sha 046f7725....

OUT2.observed: Re-running the same input kept exactly one set per binding: register-package produced 1 feature + 13 tracker_binding=none task nodes (graph_revision 5) and the second run returned idempotent=true with byte-identical receipt (binding=none local nodes, one set). The C28 beads projection created one epic B01 with B02..B14 parented to it plus 'dep add --type blocks' edges, and repeating create() left the create-call count unchanged (binding=beads bd issue/blocks edge, one set). The C27 beads claim saga confirmed a repeated same-owner claim invoked the bd bridge only once (idempotent external item). All nodes passed.

OUT3.observed: --dry-run left the graph JSON byte-equal to the pre-run state, created no receipt file, and reported write_count=0 (register-package). The C28 create --dry-run preview returned projection.write_count=0 and made zero bd calls, so binding resolution/projection diff was previewed without any local/Beads/GitHub/Projects write. All nodes passed.

OUT4.observed: Each authority conflict was fail-closed before any external write: _resolved_nodes(mode=both) with all-auto intents raised ContractError matching 'both requires'; resolving with tracker binding 'beads' while a node intent was 'github' raised ContractError matching 'not allowed' (beads + Issue/github publication conflict); and a source lineage digest mismatch raised ContractError 'lineage digest mismatch'. No node was registered and no external mutation occurred. Test passed.

OUT5.observed: After a successful local lease claim (state=claimed, execution_context_projection=applied via the C02 writer), forcing the subsequent bd claim to raise ContractError left the ledger row for the failed node in state=released with compensation=reservation_released_after_bd_claim_failure, while the earlier succeeded local claim was untouched and the same-owner re-claim stayed idempotent (bridge invoked once). The C27 fail-closed pending-release/projection-repair node confirmed only the failed operation is repaired, so local state is preserved and just the failed operation is re-runnable. Both nodes passed.

OUT6.observed: register-package registered exactly 13 task nodes under parent_feature=feature-1 with feature_package_id=feature-package/demo and expected_count=13 across phase_refs P01..P13; the second invocation returned idempotent=true with byte-identical receipt, so re-running the same ready feature keeps a single P01..P13 exact-13 set with matching parent_feature/feature_package_id. The C28 projection independently asserted expected_count==applied_count==13 with all 13 tasks parented to one epic. Both nodes passed.

OUT7.observed: A second registration presenting conflicting content for the same nodes was rejected, and tampering with the immutable receipt then re-registering raised ContractError 'immutable receipt conflicts'. Because both auto and manual routes are keyed on graph_node_id+source_digest, an identical re-registration converges to the one immutable receipt with no duplicate node, so auto and manual /system-dev-plan paths cannot double-register. Both nodes passed.
